_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Manage the data product lifecycle, including launch, active management, deprecation, and consumer migration planning.
comments: DCWF task superset of WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-T-011 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-T-005 (TF, cosine: 0.471), DQ-K-016 (DQ, cosine: 0.465).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.7/10 (raw 0.572). Shared vocabulary: [manage, product]. DCWF-only terms [releases] vs WIDAI-only terms [active, consumer, data, deprecation, launch, lifecycle] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.902 (P=0.945, R=0.864). P exceeds R by 0.081 — the DCWF element's 3 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 14 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). "Manage product releases." and "Manage the data product lifecycle, including launch, active management, deprecat" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.578 ≈ STS 0.572 (gap 0.006). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [manage, product] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.3%. Shared tokens: [manage, product]. DCWF-only: [releases]. WIDAI-only: [active, consumer, data, deprecation, launch, lifecycle].
